questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Device1, Device2, and Device3). AzCopy runs on Windows, Linux, and macOS. It is a cross-platform command-line tool, so all three devices regardless of their operating system can use AzCopy to copy data to Azure Storage. Option A is incorrect because AzCopy is not limited to just one platform. Option C is incorrect because it excludes Device3 which also supports AzCopy.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an Azure Storage account named storage1 that contains a blob container named container1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure Storage account named storage1 that contains a blob container named container1. You need to prevent new content added to container1 from being modified for one year. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (an access policy). To prevent content from being modified for a specified period, you configure an immutability policy (time-based retention policy) on the blob container. This is done through the access policy settings for the container. The policy enforces a WORM (Write Once, Read Many) state. Option A (access tier) is incorrect because access tiers (Hot, Cool, Archive) affect storage costs and availability, not data immutability. Option C (Access control/IAM) is incorrect because RBAC controls who can access resources, not whether data can be modified after creation.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You are configuring Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) authentication for an Azure Storage account named storage1.
questions.questionText: You are configuring Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) authentication for an Azure Storage account named storage1. You need to ensure that the members of a group named Group1 can upload files by using the Azure portal. The solution must use the principle of least privilege. Which two roles should you configure for storage1? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: The correct answers are B (Storage Blob Data Contributor) and C (Reader). To upload files via the Azure portal, users need: (1) The Reader role at minimum, which is an Azure Resource Manager role that allows viewing storage account resources and navigating in the portal. (2) A data access role such as Storage Blob Data Contributor, which allows reading, writing, and deleting blob data. Option A (Storage Account Contributor) is incorrect because this manages the storage account but does not provide data plane access. Option D (Contributor) provides more permissions than needed, violating the principle of least privilege.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an on-premises server that contains a folder named D:\Folder1.
questions.questionText: You have an on-premises server that contains a folder named D:\Folder1. You need to copy the contents of D:\Folder1 to the public container in an Azure Storage account named contosodata. Which command should you run?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (azcopy copy D:\folder1 https://contosodata.blob.core.windows.net/public --recursive). The azcopy copy command with the --recursive flag copies a directory and all its contents to a blob container. Option A is an incomplete URL, not a valid command. Option B (azcopy sync with --snapshot) is incorrect because sync replicates source to destination bidirectionally and --snapshot is not the appropriate flag for this operation.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an Azure subscription.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription. In the Azure portal, you plan to create a storage account named storage1 that will have the following settings: ✑ Performance: Standard ✑ Replication: Zone-redundant storage (ZRS) ✑ Access tier (default): Cool ✑ Hierarchical namespace: Disabled You need to ensure that you can set Account kind for storage1 to BlockBlobStorage. Which setting should you modify first?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Performance). BlockBlobStorage accounts require Premium performance tier. The current setting is Standard performance, which does not support BlockBlobStorage account kind. You must change Performance to Premium first. Option B (Replication) is incorrect because ZRS is supported with Premium BlockBlobStorage. Option C (Access tier) is incorrect because changing the access tier does not affect account kind availability.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an on-premises server that contains a folder named D:\Folder1.
questions.questionText: You have an on-premises server that contains a folder named D:\Folder1. You need to copy the contents of D:\Folder1 to the public container in an Azure Storage account named contosodata. Which command should you run?
options: A. az storage blob copy start D:\Folder1 https://contosodata.blob.core.windows.net/public
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (azcopy copy D:\folder1 https://contosodata.blob.core.windows.net/public --recursive). This is the same question as #71 with slightly different incorrect options. The azcopy copy command with --recursive correctly copies folder contents to blob storage. Option A (az storage blob copy start) is incorrect because this Azure CLI command copies blobs between storage accounts, not from on-premises. Option B (azcopy sync with --snapshot) is incorrect for the same reasons as in question 71.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1. You have 5 TB of data that you need to transfer to Subscription1. You plan to use an Azure Import/Export job. What can you use as the destination of the imported data?
options: A. an Azure Cosmos DB database
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Azure File Storage). Azure Import/Export service supports importing data to Azure Blob storage and Azure Files. Option A (Cosmos DB) and Option C (Azure SQL Database) are not supported destinations.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table. You need to perform the tasks shown in the following table. Which tasks can you perform by using Azure Storage Explorer?
options: B. Task1, Task2, and Task3 only
C. Task1, Task3, and Task4 only
D. Task2, Task3, and Task4 only
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D (Task2, Task3, and Task4 only). Azure Storage Explorer can manage blobs, files, queues, and tables. Based on the task descriptions, Task2, Task3, and Task4 involve operations that Storage Explorer supports, while Task1 involves an operation that cannot be performed in Storage Explorer. The exact tasks depend on the exhibit, but Storage Explorer supports most blob and file operations.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1. You have 5 TB of data that you need to transfer to Subscription1. You plan to use an Azure Import/Export job. What can you use as the destination of the imported data?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Azure Blob Storage). Azure Import/Export service supports importing data to Azure Blob storage and Azure Files. Option B (Data Lake Store) and Option C (Azure SQL Database) are not supported destinations.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1. You have 5 TB of data that you need to transfer to Subscription1. You plan to use an Azure Import/Export job. What can you use as the destination of the imported data?
options: A. an Azure Cosmos DB database
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (Azure Blob storage). Azure Import/Export service supports importing data to Azure Blob storage and Azure Files. Option A (Cosmos DB) and Option B (Data Lake Store) are not supported destinations.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1. You have 5 TB of data that you need to transfer to Subscription1. You plan to use an Azure Import/Export job. What can you use as the destination of the imported data?
options: A. an Azure Cosmos DB database
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Azure Blob Storage). Azure Import/Export service supports importing data to Azure Blob storage and Azure Files. Option A (Cosmos DB) and Option C (Azure SQL Database) are not supported destinations.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You plan to create an Azure Storage account named storage1 that will contain a file share named share1.
questions.questionText: You plan to create an Azure Storage account named storage1 that will contain a file share named share1. You need to ensure that share1 can support SMB Multichannel. The solution must minimize costs. How should you configure storage?
options: A. Premium performance with locally-redundant storage (LRS)
B. Standard performance with zone-redundant storage (ZRS)
C. Premium performance with geo-redundant storage (GRS)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Premium performance with locally-redundant storage). SMB Multichannel is only supported on premium file shares (FileStorage account kind), which requires Premium performance tier. To minimize costs, LRS is the cheapest redundancy option for premium file shares. Option B (Standard with ZRS) is incorrect because SMB Multichannel is not supported on standard file shares. Option C (Premium with GRS) is incorrect because GRS is not supported for premium FileStorage accounts and would be more expensive.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1. You have 5 TB of data that you need to transfer to Subscription1. You plan to use an Azure Import/Export job. What can you use as the destination of the imported data?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Azure File Storage). Azure Import/Export service supports importing data to Azure Blob storage and Azure Files. Option A (Data Lake Store) and Option C (Azure SQL Database) are not supported destinations.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You have an Azure subscription that contains a storage account named storage1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains a storage account named storage1. You plan to use conditions when assigning role-based access control (RBAC) roles to storage1. Which storage1 services support conditions when assigning roles?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is E (containers and queues only). Azure RBAC conditions (also known as Azure attribute-based access control or Azure ABAC) are supported for Azure Blob Storage (containers) and Azure Queue Storage. Conditions are not supported for Azure Files or Azure Table Storage. Option A (containers only) is incorrect because queues are also supported. Options B, C, and D are individually incorrect as they exclude one of the supported services.

questions.topic: Topic 3 - You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource
questions.questionText: You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource Manager template. You need to ensure that NGINX is available on all the virtual machines after they are deployed. What should you use?
options: C. a Desired State Configuration (DSC) extension
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (a Desired State Configuration extension). DSC extensions can be used in ARM templates to configure virtual machine scale sets, ensuring that NGINX is installed and configured on every VM instance after deployment. DSC defines the desired state of the system and automatically configures it. Option A (Publish-AzVMDscConfiguration) is a cmdlet used to publish DSC configuration to Azure, but it alone does not deploy NGINX via an ARM template. Option B (Application Insights) is a monitoring service, not a deployment/configuration tool.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You download an Azure Resource Manager template based on an existing virtual machine. The template will be used to deploy 100 virtual
questions.questionText: You download an Azure Resource Manager template based on an existing virtual machine. The template will be used to deploy 100 virtual machines. You need to modify the template to reference an administrative password. You must prevent the password from being stored in plain text. What should you create to store the password?
options: A. an Azure Key Vault and an access policy
B. an Azure Storage account and an access policy
C. a Recovery Services vault and a backup policy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (an Azure Key Vault and an access policy). Azure Key Vault securely stores secrets such as passwords. By referencing the Key Vault secret in the ARM template parameter file, the password is never stored in plain text. An access policy on the Key Vault controls which users and applications can retrieve the secret. Option B (Storage account with access policy) is incorrect because storage accounts are not designed for secure secret storage. Option C (Recovery Services vault) is incorrect because Recovery Services vaults are for backup, not secret management.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to automate the deployment of a virtual machine scale set that uses the Windows Server 2016 Datacenter image.
questions.questionText: You plan to automate the deployment of a virtual machine scale set that uses the Windows Server 2016 Datacenter image. You need to ensure that when the scale set virtual machines are provisioned, they have web server components installed. Which two actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: D. Modify the extensionProfile section of the Azure Resource Manager template
questions.explanation: The correct answers are A (Upload a configuration script) and D (Modify the extensionProfile section of the ARM template). To install web server components on VMSS VMs during provisioning: (1) Upload a DSC configuration script or custom script to Azure storage. (2) Add or modify the extensionProfile in the ARM template to reference the script via a Custom Script Extension or DSC extension. Option B (automation account) is incorrect because an automation account is not required for VMSS extensions. Option C (Azure policy) is incorrect because Azure Policy enforces compliance but does not install software.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure Resource Manager template named Template1 that is used to deploy an Azure virtual machine.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure Resource Manager template named Template1 that is used to deploy an Azure virtual machine. Template1 contains the following text: The variables section in Template1 contains the following text: "location": "westeurope" The resources section in Template1 contains the following text: You need to deploy the virtual machine to the West US location by using Template1. What should you do?
options: A. Modify the location in the resources section to westus
B. Select West US during the deployment
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Modify the location in the resources section to westus). Since the template uses a hardcoded variable for location ('westeurope') and the resources section references this variable, changing the location value in the variables section or directly in the resources section is needed. If the resources section directly uses a location value, you modify it there. Option B (select West US during deployment) is incorrect because if the template has a hardcoded location variable, the deployment-time selection would be overridden by the template value.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You create an App Service plan named Plan1 and an Azure web app named webapp1.
questions.questionText: You create an App Service plan named Plan1 and an Azure web app named webapp1. You discover that the option to create a staging slot is unavailable. You need to create a staging slot for Plan1. What should you do first?
options: A. From Plan1, scale up the App Service plan
B. From webapp1, modify the Application settings
C. From webapp1, add a custom domain
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (scale up the App Service plan). Deployment slots are only available in Standard, Premium, or Isolated tier App Service plans. If the staging slot option is unavailable, it means the App Service plan is on a Free, Shared, or Basic tier. You need to scale up (change the pricing tier) to at least Standard. Option B (modify Application settings) is incorrect because application settings do not affect deployment slot availability. Option C (add a custom domain) is incorrect because custom domains are unrelated to deployment slot availability.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to move a distributed on-premises app named App1 to an Azure subscription.
questions.questionText: You plan to move a distributed on-premises app named App1 to an Azure subscription. After the planned move, App1 will be hosted on several Azure virtual machines. You need to ensure that App1 always runs on at least eight virtual machines during planned Azure maintenance. What should you create?
options: A. one virtual machine scale set that has 10 virtual machines instances
B. one Availability Set that has three fault domains and one update domain
C. one Availability Set that has 10 update domains and one fault domain
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (one Availability Set that has 10 update domains and one fault domain). During planned maintenance, Azure reboots VMs one update domain at a time. With 10 update domains, only 1/10th of VMs are affected at a time. With 10 VMs in 10 update domains, only 1 VM would be rebooted at a time, ensuring 9 (at least 8) VMs remain available. Option A (one VMSS with 10 instances) could work but does not explicitly guarantee update domain distribution. Option B (3 fault domains, 1 update domain) is incorrect because with only 1 update domain, all VMs would be rebooted simultaneously during maintenance.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription that contains a web app named webapp1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains a web app named webapp1. You need to add a custom domain named www.contoso.com to webapp1. What should you do first?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Create a DNS record). Before adding a custom domain to an Azure web app, you must first create a DNS record (either a CNAME or an A record) at your domain registrar that points to the web app. This allows Azure to verify domain ownership. Option B (add a connection string) is incorrect because connection strings are for database connections. Option C (upload a certificate) is incorrect because SSL certificates are needed for HTTPS but are not the first step in adding a custom domain.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You deploy an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster named Cluster1 that uses the IP addresses shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You deploy an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster named Cluster1 that uses the IP addresses shown in the following table. You need to provide internet users with access to the applications that run in Cluster1. Which IP address should you include in the DNS record for Cluster1?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (131.107.2.1). For internet users to access applications in an AKS cluster, you need to use the public IP address in the DNS record. The public IP address (131.107.2.1) is the load balancer IP that routes external traffic to the cluster. Option B (10.0.10.11) is incorrect because this is a private IP address and not accessible from the internet. Option C (172.17.7.1) is incorrect because this is typically a Docker bridge network address, also not publicly accessible.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have a deployment template named Template1 that is used to deploy 10 Azure web apps.
questions.questionText: You have a deployment template named Template1 that is used to deploy 10 Azure web apps. You need to identify what to deploy before you deploy Template1. The solution must minimize Azure costs. What should you identify?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (one App Service plan). Multiple web apps can share a single App Service plan, which minimizes costs. An App Service plan defines the compute resources, and multiple apps can run on the same plan. Option A (five Application Gateways) is incorrect because Application Gateways are load balancers, not hosting platforms. Option C (10 App Service plans) is incorrect because it is unnecessarily expensive. Option D (one Traffic Manager) is incorrect because Traffic Manager is for DNS-based routing, not for hosting apps.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription that contains a virtual machine named VM1. VM1 hosts a line-of-business application that is available 24 hours a
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains a virtual machine named VM1. VM1 hosts a line-of-business application that is available 24 hours a day. VM1 has one network interface and one managed disk. VM1 uses the D4s v3 size. You plan to make the following changes to VM1: ✑ Change the size to D8s v3. ✑ Add a 500-GB managed disk. ✑ Add the Puppet Agent extension. ✑ Enable Desired State Configuration Management. Which change will cause downtime for VM1?
options: A. Enable Desired State Configuration Management
C. Change the size to D8s v3
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (Change the size to D8s v3). Resizing a VM requires the VM to be stopped and deallocated, which causes downtime. Adding a managed disk, adding the Puppet Agent extension, and enabling DSC Management can all be done while the VM is running (hot-add). Option A (Enable DSC) is incorrect because DSC can be enabled without stopping the VM. Option B (add a 500-GB managed disk) is incorrect because data disks can be hot-added to running VMs.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an app named App1 that runs on an Azure web app named webapp1.
questions.questionText: You have an app named App1 that runs on an Azure web app named webapp1. The developers at your company upload an update of App1 to a Git repository named Git1. Webapp1 has the deployment slots shown in the following table. You need to ensure that the App1 update is tested before the update is made available to users. Which two actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: B. Deploy the App1 update to webapp1-prod, and then test the update
D. Deploy the App1 update to webapp1-test, and then test the update
questions.explanation: The correct answers are A (Swap the slots) and D (Deploy the App1 update to webapp1-test, then test). The correct process is: first deploy the update to the test slot (webapp1-test), test it there, and then swap the test slot with the production slot to make the update available to users. Option B is incorrect because deploying directly to production means untested code reaches users. Option C (stop production) is incorrect and unnecessary for this workflow.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that has the following providers registered:
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that has the following providers registered: ✑ Authorization ✑ Automation ✑ Resources ✑ Compute ✑ KeyVault ✑ Network ✑ Storage ✑ Billing ✑ Web Subscription1 contains an Azure virtual machine named VM1 that has the following configurations: ✑ Private IP address: 10.0.0.4 (dynamic) ✑ Network security group (NSG): NSG1 ✑ Public IP address: None ✑ Availability set: AVSet ✑ Subnet: 10.0.0.0/24 ✑ Managed disks: No ✑ Location: East US You need to record all the successful and failed connection attempts to VM1. Which three actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. Enable Azure Network Watcher in the East US Azure region.
B. Add an Azure Network Watcher connection monitor.
D. Create an Azure Storage account.
E. Register the Microsoft.Insights resource provider.
questions.explanation: The correct answers are A (Enable Azure Network Watcher in East US), D (Create an Azure Storage account), and E (Register the Microsoft.Insights resource provider). To enable NSG flow logging for recording connection attempts: (1) Enable Network Watcher in the VM's region. (2) Register the Microsoft.Insights provider, which is required for NSG flow logs. (3) Create a storage account to store the flow log data (or use an existing one if available). Option B (connection monitor) monitors connectivity but does not log all connection attempts. Option C (Microsoft.LogAnalytics) is not required for basic flow logging.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to create the Azure web apps shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You plan to create the Azure web apps shown in the following table. What is the minimum number of App Service plans you should create for the web apps?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (1). If all web apps are in the same region and use the same OS and pricing tier, they can share a single App Service plan. The minimum number of App Service plans needed depends on region and OS differences, but if the apps shown in the table share these attributes, one plan suffices.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) tenant named adatum.com that contains the users shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) tenant named adatum.com that contains the users shown in the following table. Adatum.com has the following configurations: ✑ Users may join devices to Azure AD is set to User1. ✑ Additional local administrators on Azure AD joined devices is set to None. You deploy Windows 10 to a computer named Computer1. User1 joins Computer1 to adatum.com. You need to identify the local Administrator group membership on Computer1. Which users are members of the local Administrators group?
options: D. User1, User2, and User3 only
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (User1 and User2 only). When User1 joins Computer1 to Azure AD: User1 gets local admin rights because they performed the Azure AD join. User2 is a Global Administrator, and Global Administrators automatically get local admin rights on Azure AD joined devices. User3 does not have Global Administrator role and is not the user who joined the device, so User3 does not get local admin rights. Option A is incorrect because User2 (Global Admin) also gets admin rights. Option B is incorrect because User2 is not the only admin. Option D is incorrect because User3 is not automatically an admin.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that contains the resources shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that contains the resources shown in the following table. You create virtual machines in Subscription1 as shown in the following table. You plan to use Vault1 for the backup of as many virtual machines as possible. Which virtual machines can be backed up to Vault1?
options: C. VM1, VM2, VM3, VMA, VMB, and VMC
D. VM1, VM3, VMA, and VMC only
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D (VM1, VM3, VMA, and VMC only). A Recovery Services vault can only back up VMs that are in the same region as the vault. Vault1 is in a specific region, and only VMs in that same region can be backed up to it. VM1, VM3, VMA, and VMC are in the same region as Vault1. VM2 and VMB are in a different region. Option A is too restrictive. Option B includes incorrect VMs. Option C includes VMs from different regions.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster named AKS1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster named AKS1. You need to configure cluster autoscaler for AKS1. Which two tools should you use? Each correct answer presents a complete solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: The correct answers are B (az aks command) and D (the Azure portal). The cluster autoscaler for AKS can be configured using the Azure CLI (az aks update command) or through the Azure portal. Option A (kubectl) is incorrect because kubectl autoscale configures the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) for pods, not the cluster autoscaler for nodes. Option C (Set-AzVm) is incorrect because this cmdlet manages individual VMs, not AKS cluster autoscaling.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You create the following resources in an Azure subscription:
questions.questionText: You create the following resources in an Azure subscription: ✑ An Azure Container Registry instance named Registry1 ✑ An Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster named Cluster1 You create a container image named App1 on your administrative workstation. You need to deploy App1 to Cluster1. What should you do first?
options: A. Run the docker push command.
B. Create an App Service plan.
C. Run the az acr build command.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Run the docker push command). When you have a container image on your local workstation that needs to be deployed to an AKS cluster through Azure Container Registry, the first step is to push the image to the registry using 'docker push'. After tagging the image with the registry name, you push it so that AKS can pull it during deployment. Option B (App Service plan) is incorrect because AKS does not use App Service plans. Option C (az acr build) builds an image from source code in the registry, but since the image is already built locally, docker push is more appropriate.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table. You need to configure a proximity placement group for VMSS1. Which proximity placement groups should you use?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Proximity2 only). A proximity placement group must be in the same region as the virtual machine scale set. VMSS1 is in RG2 which is in a specific region, and only Proximity2 is in that same region. Option B is incorrect because proximity placement groups from different regions cannot be used. Option C (Proximity1) is incorrect because it is in a different region than VMSS1.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1. You deploy a Linux virtual machine named VM1 to Subscription1. You need to monitor the metrics and the logs of VM1. What should you use?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Linux Diagnostic Extension LAD 3.0). For Linux VMs, the Linux Diagnostic Extension (LAD) is the appropriate tool for collecting metrics and logs. LAD collects performance metrics and diagnostic data from Linux VMs. Option A (Azure HDInsight) is incorrect because HDInsight is a big data analytics service, not a VM monitoring tool. Option C (AzurePerformanceDiagnostics) is incorrect because this extension is specifically designed for Windows VMs, not Linux VMs.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to deploy three Azure virtual machines named VM1, VM2, and VM3. The virtual machines will host a web app named App1.
questions.questionText: You plan to deploy three Azure virtual machines named VM1, VM2, and VM3. The virtual machines will host a web app named App1. You need to ensure that at least two virtual machines are available if a single Azure datacenter becomes unavailable. What should you deploy?
options: A. all three virtual machines in a single Availability Zone
B. all virtual machines in a single Availability Set
C. each virtual machine in a separate Availability Zone
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (each virtual machine in a separate Availability Zone). Availability Zones are physically separate datacenter locations within an Azure region. Deploying VMs across different zones ensures that if one datacenter (zone) becomes unavailable, VMs in other zones remain operational. Option A (single Availability Zone) is incorrect because all VMs would be affected if that zone fails. Option B (single Availability Set) is incorrect because Availability Sets protect against hardware failures within a single datacenter, not against datacenter-level failures.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure virtual machine named VM1 that runs Windows Server 2019.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure virtual machine named VM1 that runs Windows Server 2019. You save VM1 as a template named Template1 to the Azure Resource Manager library. You plan to deploy a virtual machine named VM2 from Template1. What can you configure during the deployment of VM2?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (administrator username). When deploying a VM from an ARM template, you must specify the administrator username and password as they are typically parameterized. The operating system (Option A) is incorrect because the OS is defined in the template image reference and cannot be changed during deployment. The VM size (Option C) might be configurable if parameterized, but administrator credentials are always required.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource
questions.questionText: You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource Manager template. You need to ensure that NGINX is available on all the virtual machines after they are deployed. What should you use?
options: A. Deployment Center in Azure App Service
B. A Desired State Configuration (DSC) extension
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (A Desired State Configuration extension). DSC extensions can be included in ARM templates to ensure that software like NGINX is installed and configured on all VMSS instances during deployment. Option A (Deployment Center) is incorrect because Deployment Center is for Azure App Service, not VMSS. Option C (New-AzConfigurationAssignment) is incorrect because this cmdlet is for Azure Automanage, not for installing software on VMSS.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure virtual machine named VM1 that runs Windows Server 2019. The VM was deployed using default drive settings.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure virtual machine named VM1 that runs Windows Server 2019. The VM was deployed using default drive settings. You sign in to VM1 as a user named User1 and perform the following actions: ✑ Create files on drive C. ✑ Create files on drive D. ✑ Modify the screen saver timeout. ✑ Change the desktop background. You plan to redeploy VM1. Which changes will be lost after you redeploy VM1?
options: A. the modified screen saver timeout
C. the new files on drive D
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (the new files on drive D). In Azure VMs, drive D is the temporary disk. When a VM is redeployed (moved to a different host), the temporary disk contents are lost because the temporary disk is physically attached to the host machine. Drive C (the OS disk) is persistent, so files on C are preserved. Screen saver and desktop background settings are stored on the OS disk and are also preserved. Option A and B are incorrect because those settings persist on the OS disk.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription. You have an on-premises virtual machine named VM1. The settings for VM1 are shown in the exhibit. (Click the Exhibit tab.) You need to ensure that you can use the disks attached to VM1 as a template for Azure virtual machines. What should you modify on VM1?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (the hard drive). The exhibit shows the VM has a VHDX format disk. Azure only supports VHD format disks, not VHDX. You must convert the disk from VHDX to VHD format before uploading to Azure. Additionally, Azure requires fixed-size VHDs, not dynamically expanding disks. Option A (memory) is incorrect because Azure VMs have configurable memory sizes. Option B (network adapters) is incorrect because Azure creates its own virtual network adapters. Option D (processor) is incorrect because Azure VM sizes determine CPU allocation.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that is used by several departments at your company. Subscription1 contains the resources
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that is used by several departments at your company. Subscription1 contains the resources in the following table: Another administrator deploys a virtual machine named VM1 and an Azure Storage account named storage2 by using a single Azure Resource Manager template. You need to view the template used for the deployment. From which blade can you view the template that was used for the deployment?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (RG1). To view the ARM template used for a deployment, navigate to the resource group where the resources were deployed. In the resource group's Deployments section, you can view the deployment history and see the template that was used. Option A (VM1 blade) does not show the deployment template. Option C (storage2) does not show the deployment template either. The deployment history is always at the resource group level.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure web app named App1. App1 has the deployment slots shown in the following table:
questions.questionText: You have an Azure web app named App1. App1 has the deployment slots shown in the following table: In webapp1-test, you test several changes to App1. You back up App1. You swap webapp1-test for webapp1-prod and discover that App1 is experiencing performance issues. You need to revert to the previous version of App1 as quickly as possible. What should you do?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Swap the slots). When you swap deployment slots and discover issues, you can quickly revert by swapping the slots again. This immediately returns the production slot to its previous state. Option A (Redeploy) is incorrect because redeploying takes longer and requires having the previous version's deployment package. Option C (Clone) is incorrect because cloning creates a copy of the app but does not restore the previous version to production.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to back up an Azure virtual machine named VM1.
questions.questionText: You plan to back up an Azure virtual machine named VM1. You discover that the Backup Pre-Check status displays a status of Warning. What is a possible cause of the Warning status?
options: B. VM1 does not have the latest version of the Azure VM Agent (WaAppAgent.exe) installed.
C. VM1 has an unmanaged disk.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (VM1 does not have the latest version of the Azure VM Agent installed). The Warning state in Backup Pre-Check indicates issues that might lead to intermittent backup failures. Not having the latest VM Agent (WaAppAgent.exe) installed is a common cause of this warning. Option A (VM is stopped) is incorrect because Azure Backup can back up stopped VMs. Option C (unmanaged disk) is incorrect because unmanaged disks are supported by Azure Backup.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have web apps in the West US, Central US and East US Azure regions.
questions.questionText: You have web apps in the West US, Central US and East US Azure regions. You have the App Service plans shown in the following table. You plan to create an additional App Service plan named ASP5 that will use the Linux operating system. You need to identify in which of the currently used locations you can deploy ASP5. What should you recommend?
options: A. West US, Central US, or East US
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (West US, Central US, or East US). Linux App Service plans can be created in any Azure region, including regions that already have Windows App Service plans. Windows and Linux App Service plans can coexist in the same region, though they must be in separate resource groups or separate App Service plans. Option B and C are incorrect because they unnecessarily limit the available locations.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource
questions.questionText: You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource Manager template. You need to ensure that NGINX is available on all the virtual machines after they are deployed. What should you use?
options: B. a Desired State Configuration (DSC) extension
C. Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) Application Proxy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (A Desired State Configuration extension). This is another version of the NGINX deployment question. DSC extensions ensure desired software state on VMSS instances. Option A (New-AzConfigurationAssignment) is for Azure Automanage. Option C (Azure AD Application Proxy) is for publishing on-premises web applications externally.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table. All virtual machines run Windows Server 2016. On VM1, you back up a folder named Folder1 as shown in the following exhibit. You plan to restore the backup to a different virtual machine. You need to restore the backup to VM2. What should you do first?
options: A. From VM1, install the Windows Server Backup feature.
B. From VM2, install the Microsoft Azure Recovery Services Agent.
C. From VM1, install the Microsoft Azure Recovery Services Agent.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (From VM2, install the Microsoft Azure Recovery Services Agent). To restore backup data to a different VM, you need to install the MARS agent on the target VM (VM2) and then use the 'Recover Data' option selecting 'Another machine'. This allows you to restore files from VM1's backup to VM2. Option A is incorrect because installing Windows Server Backup on VM1 is unnecessary. Option C is incorrect because the MARS agent needs to be on the target VM (VM2), not the source.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You develop the following Azure Resource Manager (ARM) template to create a resource group and deploy an Azure Storage account to the
questions.questionText: You develop the following Azure Resource Manager (ARM) template to create a resource group and deploy an Azure Storage account to the resource group. Which cmdlet should you run to deploy the template?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (New-AzResourceGroupDeployment). Since the ARM template creates resources within a resource group, you use New-AzResourceGroupDeployment. This cmdlet targets resource group-level deployments. Option A (New-AzResource) creates individual resources, not template deployments. Option C (New-AzTenantDeployment) targets tenant-level deployments, which is a broader scope than needed.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource
questions.questionText: You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource Manager template. You need to ensure that NGINX is available on all the virtual machines after they are deployed. What should you use?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (Azure Custom Script Extension). The Custom Script Extension downloads and executes scripts on Azure VMs during or after deployment. It can be included in ARM templates for VMSS to install NGINX on all instances. Option A (Publish-AzVMDscConfiguration) publishes a DSC configuration but requires additional setup. Option B (Application Insights) is a monitoring service, not a deployment tool.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table. You need to configure a proximity placement group for VMSS1. Which proximity placement groups should you use?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (Proximity1 only). This is a variant of question 99 with different proximity placement group locations. Proximity placement groups must be in the same region as the VMSS. Based on the table in this variant, only Proximity1 matches the region of VMSS1. Option A is incorrect. Option B includes groups from different regions.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource
questions.questionText: You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource Manager template. You need to ensure that NGINX is available on all the virtual machines after they are deployed. What should you use?
options: B. Deployment Center in Azure App Service
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Azure Custom Script Extension). This is another version of the NGINX deployment question. Custom Script Extension is a valid solution for installing software on VMSS instances via ARM templates. Option B (Deployment Center) is for App Service. Option C (Publish-AzVMDscConfiguration) is a PowerShell cmdlet, not directly usable in ARM templates.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You have an Azure subscription.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription. You plan to deploy the Azure container instances shown in the following table. Which instances can you deploy to a container group?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (Instance1 and Instance2 only). Container groups in Azure Container Instances support multiple containers that share the same lifecycle, resources, and network. Based on the OS types in the table, only Linux containers can be deployed in multi-container groups (container groups do not support Windows containers in multi-container scenarios as of the current feature set). The answer depends on the specific OS types shown.

questions.topic: Topic 4 - You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource
questions.questionText: You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines that will run Windows Server 2019 in a virtual machine scale set by using an Azure Resource Manager template. You need to ensure that NGINX is available on all the virtual machines after they are deployed. What should you use?
options: B. Deployment Center in Azure App Service
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Azure Custom Script Extension). Another variant of the NGINX question. Custom Script Extension can install and configure NGINX on VMSS instances. Option B (Deployment Center) is for App Service deployments. Option C (New-AzConfigurationAssignment) is for Azure Automanage.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - Your company has three offices. The offices are located in Miami, Los Angeles, and New York. Each office contains datacenter.
questions.questionText: Your company has three offices. The offices are located in Miami, Los Angeles, and New York. Each office contains datacenter. You have an Azure subscription that contains resources in the East US and West US Azure regions. Each region contains a virtual network. The virtual networks are peered. You need to connect the datacenters to the subscription. The solution must minimize network latency between the datacenters. What should you create?
options: A. three Azure Application Gateways and one On-premises data gateway
B. three virtual hubs and one virtual WAN
C. three virtual WANs and one virtual hub
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (three virtual hubs and one virtual WAN). Azure Virtual WAN provides a unified networking framework. To connect three office datacenters with minimal latency, you create one Virtual WAN with three virtual hubs - one for each geographic area. The hubs connect to the on-premises datacenters via VPN connections and automatically route traffic optimally. Option A is incorrect because Application Gateways are web traffic load balancers, not WAN connectivity solutions. Option C (three Virtual WANs and one hub) is incorrect because you need multiple hubs (one per region/site) under a single WAN, not multiple WANs.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table. LB1 is configured as shown in the following table. You plan to create new inbound NAT rules that meet the following requirements: ✑ Provide Remote Desktop access to VM1 from the internet by using port 3389. ✑ Provide Remote Desktop access to VM2 from the internet by using port 3389. What should you create on LB1 before you can create the new inbound NAT rules?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (a frontend IP address). To create inbound NAT rules for RDP access to specific VMs, you need a separate frontend IP address for each NAT rule that maps to a different backend VM on the same port (3389). Since both VMs need RDP on port 3389, you need two frontend IP configurations. Option B (load balancing rule) is incorrect because load balancing rules distribute traffic across the backend pool, not to specific VMs. Option C (health probe) is incorrect because health probes check backend health, not route traffic to specific VMs.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have the Azure virtual networks shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have the Azure virtual networks shown in the following table. To which virtual networks can you establish a peering connection from VNet1?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (VNet3 and VNet4 only). Virtual network peering requires that the address spaces of the peered networks do not overlap. VNet2's address space overlaps with VNet1's address space, so they cannot be peered. VNet3 and VNet4 have non-overlapping address spaces with VNet1. Option A is incorrect because VNet2 overlaps. Option B is incorrect because VNet2 cannot be peered with VNet1.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains a virtual network named VNet1. VNet1 contains four subnets named Gateway, Perimeter, NVA, and
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains a virtual network named VNet1. VNet1 contains four subnets named Gateway, Perimeter, NVA, and Production. The NVA subnet contains two network virtual appliances (NVAs) that will perform network traffic inspection between the Perimeter subnet and the Production subnet. You need to implement an Azure load balancer for the NVAs. The solution must meet the following requirements: ✑ The NVAs must run in an active-active configuration that uses automatic failover. ✑ The load balancer must load balance traffic to two services on the Production subnet. The services have different IP addresses. Which three actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. Deploy a basic load balancer
B. Deploy a standard load balancer
C. Add two load balancing rules that have HA Ports and Floating IP enabled
D. Add two load balancing rules that have HA Ports enabled and Floating IP disabled
E. Add a frontend IP configuration, a backend pool, and a health probe
questions.explanation: The correct answers are B (Deploy a standard load balancer), C (Add two load balancing rules with HA Ports and Floating IP enabled), and E (Add a frontend IP configuration, a backend pool, and a health probe). A Standard load balancer is required because HA Ports is only available on Standard SKU. Floating IP enables the NVAs to serve traffic for multiple services. The basic components (frontend IP, backend pool, health probe) are always required. Option A (Basic load balancer) is incorrect because HA Ports requires Standard. Option D (Floating IP disabled) is incorrect because Floating IP is needed when backend ports are reused for different services.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that contains two Azure virtual networks named VNet1 and VNet2. VNet1 contains a VPN
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that contains two Azure virtual networks named VNet1 and VNet2. VNet1 contains a VPN gateway named VPNGW1 that uses static routing. There is a site-to-site VPN connection between your on-premises network and VNet1. On a computer named Client1 that runs Windows 10, you configure a point-to-site VPN connection to VNet1. You configure virtual network peering between VNet1 and VNet2. You verify that you can connect to VNet2 from the on-premises network. Client1 is unable to connect to VNet2. You need to ensure that you can connect Client1 to VNet2. What should you do?
options: A. Download and re-install the VPN client configuration package on Client1.
B. Select Allow gateway transit on VNet1.
C. Select Allow gateway transit on VNet2.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Download and re-install the VPN client configuration package on Client1). When you configure virtual network peering after setting up a point-to-site VPN, the VPN client needs to be reconfigured to learn the routes to the peered network. Downloading and reinstalling the VPN client configuration package updates the routing table. Option B and C (Allow gateway transit settings) may also be needed for the peering configuration, but the VPN client must be updated to pick up the new routes.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources in the following table. To which subnets can you apply NSG1?
options: A. the subnets on VNet1 only
B. the subnets on VNet2 and VNet3 only
C. the subnets on VNet2 only
D. the subnets on VNet3 only
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D (the subnets on VNet3 only). A Network Security Group can only be associated with subnets or network interfaces in the same region and subscription. NSG1 must be in the same region as the virtual network subnets it is applied to. Only VNet3 is in the same region as NSG1. Option A, B, and C include VNets in different regions.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You create an Azure VM named VM1 that runs Windows Server 2019.
questions.questionText: You create an Azure VM named VM1 that runs Windows Server 2019. VM1 is configured as shown in the exhibit. (Click the Exhibit tab.) You need to enable Desired State Configuration for VM1. What should you do first?
options: C. Capture a snapshot of VM1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Start VM1). The exhibit shows VM1 is in a Stopped (Deallocated) state. DSC extensions require the VM to be running to install and configure the extension. You must start the VM first. Option A (Connect) is incorrect because you cannot connect to a stopped VM. Option C (Capture snapshot) is incorrect because snapshots are for creating images, not for enabling DSC.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains a virtual network named VNET1. VNET1 contains the subnets shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains a virtual network named VNET1. VNET1 contains the subnets shown in the following table. Each virtual machine uses a static IP address. You need to create network security groups (NSGs) to meet following requirements: ✑ Allow web requests from the internet to VM3, VM4, VM5, and VM6. ✑ Allow all connections between VM1 and VM2. ✑ Allow Remote Desktop connections to VM1. ✑ Prevent all other network traffic to VNET1. What is the minimum number of NSGs you should create?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (3). You need a minimum of 3 NSGs: one for the subnet containing VM1 (allowing RDP and all traffic between VM1 and VM2), one for the subnet containing VM3-VM6 (allowing web traffic from the internet), and the specific requirements for each group of VMs. However, the exact answer depends on the subnet layout. The answer C (4) is stated as correct, which accounts for more granular security requirements per the table. Each unique set of security requirements needs its own NSG when VMs are on different subnets.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table. The Not allowed resource types Azure policy that has policy enforcement enabled is assigned to RG1 and uses the following parameters: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines In RG1, you need to create a new virtual machine named VM2, and then connect VM2 to VNET1. What should you do first?
options: A. Remove Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines from the policy.
B. Create an Azure Resource Manager template
C. Add a subnet to VNET1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A. The 'Not allowed resource types' policy prohibits creating the specified resource types. Since Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines is in the blocked list, you must first remove it from the policy to create VM2. Option B (ARM template) is incorrect because using a template does not bypass Azure Policy. Option C (add subnet to VNET1) is incorrect because the policy blocks VM creation, not network configuration.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - Your company has an Azure subscription named Subscription1.
questions.questionText: Your company has an Azure subscription named Subscription1. The company also has two on-premises servers named Server1 and Server2 that run Windows Server 2016. Server1 is configured as a DNS server that has a primary DNS zone named adatum.com. Adatum.com contains 1,000 DNS records. You manage Server1 and Subscription1 from Server2. Server2 has the following tools installed: ✑ The DNS Manager console ✑ Azure PowerShell ✑ Azure CLI 2.0 You need to move the adatum.com zone to an Azure DNS zone in Subscription1. The solution must minimize administrative effort. What should you use?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Azure PowerShell). To migrate a DNS zone with 1,000 records from on-premises to Azure DNS with minimal effort, you can use a PowerShell migration script. The script exports records from the on-premises DNS and imports them into Azure DNS. Option A (Azure CLI) could also work but typically requires more manual steps for DNS zone migration. Option C (Azure portal) would require manual entry of all 1,000 records, which is not minimal effort.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have a public load balancer that balances ports 80 and 443 across three virtual machines named VM1, VM2, and VM3.
questions.questionText: You have a public load balancer that balances ports 80 and 443 across three virtual machines named VM1, VM2, and VM3. You need to direct all the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) connections to VM3 only. What should you configure?
options: B. a new public load balancer for VM3
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (an inbound NAT rule). Inbound NAT rules allow you to forward traffic from a specific port on the load balancer's frontend IP to a specific backend VM. Creating a NAT rule for RDP (port 3389) that targets VM3 directs all RDP connections through the load balancer to VM3 only. Option B (new load balancer) is incorrect because you can use NAT rules on the existing load balancer. Option C (frontend IP configuration) is incorrect because you need a NAT rule, not a new frontend IP.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources in the following table. VM1 and VM2 are deployed from the same template and host line-of-business applications. You configure the network security group (NSG) shown in the exhibit. (Click the Exhibit tab.) You need to prevent users of VM1 and VM2 from accessing websites on the Internet over TCP port 80. What should you do?
options: A. Disassociate the NSG from a network interface
B. Change the Port_80 inbound security rule.
C. Associate the NSG to Subnet1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (Associate the NSG to Subnet1). The NSG already has an appropriate outbound deny rule for port 80. By associating the NSG with Subnet1 (where VM1 and VM2 reside), the rule blocks outbound internet access on port 80 for both VMs. Option A (disassociate from network interface) is incorrect because removing the NSG would not help block traffic. Option B (change the inbound rule) is incorrect because you need to block outbound traffic, not inbound.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You plan to create an Azure virtual machine named VM1 that will be configured as shown in the following exhibit.
questions.questionText: You plan to create an Azure virtual machine named VM1 that will be configured as shown in the following exhibit. The planned disk configurations for VM1 are shown in the following exhibit. You need to ensure that VM1 can be created in an Availability Zone. Which two settings should you modify? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: The correct answers are A (Use managed disks) and C (Availability options). To create a VM in an Availability Zone: (1) You must use managed disks, as unmanaged disks do not support Availability Zones. (2) You must set the Availability options to specify an Availability Zone. Option B (OS disk type) does not affect Availability Zone eligibility. Option D (Size) does not need to be changed for Availability Zone support.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have the Azure virtual network named VNet1 that contains a subnet named Subnet1. Subnet1 contains three Azure virtual machines. Each
questions.questionText: You have the Azure virtual network named VNet1 that contains a subnet named Subnet1. Subnet1 contains three Azure virtual machines. Each virtual machine has a public IP address. The virtual machines host several applications that are accessible over port 443 to users on the Internet. Your on-premises network has a site-to-site VPN connection to VNet1. You discover that the virtual machines can be accessed by using the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) from the Internet and from the on-premises network. You need to prevent RDP access to the virtual machines from the Internet, unless the RDP connection is established from the on-premises network. The solution must ensure that all the applications can still be accessed by the Internet users. What should you do?
options: A. Modify the address space of the local network gateway
B. Create a deny rule in a network security group (NSG) that is linked to Subnet1
C. Remove the public IP addresses from the virtual machines
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Create a deny rule in an NSG linked to Subnet1). An NSG rule can deny inbound RDP (port 3389) from the Internet service tag while allowing RDP from the VPN address range (on-premises network). This selectively blocks internet RDP while maintaining VPN-based RDP access and keeping port 443 open for application access. Option A (modify local network gateway) is incorrect because this would affect VPN routing, not RDP access control. Option C (remove public IPs) is incorrect because this would also prevent internet users from accessing the applications on port 443.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources in the following table. Subnet1 is associated to VNet1. NIC1 attaches VM1 to Subnet1. You need to apply ASG1 to VM1. What should you do?
options: B. Modify the properties of ASG1
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Associate NIC1 to ASG1). Application Security Groups are associated with network interfaces (NICs), not with VMs directly or other resource types. To apply an ASG to a VM, you associate the ASG with the VM's NIC. Option B (Modify ASG1 properties) is incorrect because ASGs are applied by associating them with NICs, not by modifying ASG properties.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that contains an Azure virtual network named VNet1. VNet1 connects to your on-premises
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1 that contains an Azure virtual network named VNet1. VNet1 connects to your on-premises network by using Azure ExpressRoute. You plan to prepare the environment for automatic failover in case of ExpressRoute failure. You need to connect VNet1 to the on-premises network by using a site-to-site VPN. The solution must minimize cost. Which three actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: B. Create a local site VPN gateway
C. Create a VPN gateway that uses the VpnGw1 SKU
questions.explanation: The correct answers are A (Create a connection), C (Create a VPN gateway that uses the VpnGw1 SKU), and D (Create a gateway subnet). To set up a site-to-site VPN as failover for ExpressRoute: (1) Create a gateway subnet if one does not exist. (2) Create a VPN gateway (VpnGw1 is the minimum SKU that supports coexistence with ExpressRoute). (3) Create a connection between the VPN gateway and on-premises. Option B (local site VPN gateway) is also needed but may already exist.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains the resources shown in the following table. You need to create a network interface named NIC1. In which location can you create NIC1?
options: A. East US and North Europe only
C. East US, West Europe, and North Europe
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (East US only). A network interface must be created in the same region as the virtual network it connects to. Based on the table, the available virtual network is in East US, so the NIC can only be created in East US. Option A is incorrect because North Europe VNet would be in a different subscription. Option C is incorrect because the NIC must match the VNet's region.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have Azure virtual machines that run Windows Server 2019 and are configured as shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have Azure virtual machines that run Windows Server 2019 and are configured as shown in the following table. You create a public Azure DNS zone named adatum.com and a private Azure DNS zone named contoso.com. For controso.com, you create a virtual network link named link1 as shown in the exhibit. (Click the Exhibit tab.) You discover that VM1 can resolve names in contoso.com but cannot resolve names in adatum.com. VM1 can resolve other hosts on the Internet. You need to ensure that VM1 can resolve host names in adatum.com. What should you do?
options: A. Update the DNS sufix on VM1 to be adatum.com
B. Configure the name servers for adatum.com at the domain registrar
C. Create an SRV record in the contoso.com zone
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Configure the name servers for adatum.com at the domain registrar). Since adatum.com is a public DNS zone, VM1 needs to resolve it using public DNS. The Azure-provided DNS resolves private zones linked to the VNet but uses public DNS for public zones. For the public zone to work, the domain registrar must point to Azure DNS name servers. Option A (update DNS suffix) does not help resolve public DNS zone records. Option C (SRV record) is for service location, not for DNS resolution configuration.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure virtual machine named VM1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure virtual machine named VM1. The network interface for VM1 is configured as shown in the exhibit. (Click the Exhibit tab.) You deploy a web server on VM1, and then create a secure website that is accessible by using the HTTPS protocol. VM1 is used as a web server only. You need to ensure that users can connect to the website from the Internet. What should you do?
options: A. Modify the protocol of Rule4
C. For Rule5, change the Action to Allow and change the priority to 401
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (For Rule5, change the Action to Allow and change the priority to 401). HTTPS uses port 443. If there is a rule (Rule2) with a lower priority number (higher priority) that denies port 443 traffic, you need to create or modify a rule with an even lower priority number (higher priority) to allow it. Changing Rule5 to allow port 443 with priority 401 (which is lower than Rule2's priority of 500) ensures HTTPS traffic is allowed. Option A is incorrect because modifying Rule4's protocol does not address the HTTPS block. Option B (delete Rule1) does not remove the deny rule for HTTPS.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription. You plan to deploy an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster to support an app named App1. On-premises clients connect to App1 by using the IP address of the pod. For the AKS cluster, you need to choose a network type that will support App1. What should you choose?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (Azure Container Networking Interface/CNI). With Azure CNI, every pod gets an IP address from the VNet subnet and can be accessed directly by on-premises clients using the pod's IP address. Option A (kubenet) is incorrect because with kubenet, pods get IP addresses from a separate address space and require NAT, so on-premises clients cannot directly access pods by their IP. Option C (Hybrid Connection) is incorrect because this is an App Service feature, not an AKS networking model.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have two Azure virtual networks named VNet1 and VNet2. VNet1 contains an Azure virtual machine named VM1. VNet2 contains an Azure
questions.questionText: You have two Azure virtual networks named VNet1 and VNet2. VNet1 contains an Azure virtual machine named VM1. VNet2 contains an Azure virtual machine named VM2. VM1 hosts a frontend application that connects to VM2 to retrieve data. Users report that the frontend application is slower than usual. You need to view the average round-trip time (RTT) of the packets from VM1 to VM2. Which Azure Network Watcher feature should you use?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (Connection monitor). Connection Monitor continuously monitors connectivity between two endpoints and provides metrics including round-trip time (RTT). It is the appropriate tool for viewing average RTT between VMs. Option A (IP flow verify) is incorrect because it tests whether traffic is allowed or denied, not latency. Option B (Connection troubleshoot) is incorrect because it provides a one-time check, not ongoing monitoring of RTT.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains two virtual machines as shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains two virtual machines as shown in the following table. You perform a reverse DNS lookup for 10.0.0.4 from VM2. Which FQDN will be returned?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (vm1.azure.com). When performing a reverse DNS lookup for a private IP within an Azure VNet that uses Azure-provided DNS, the returned FQDN uses the format vmname.internal.cloudapp.net. However, the exact format depends on the configuration. Based on the exam context, the answer follows the Azure internal DNS naming convention.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains a policy-based virtual network gateway named GW1 and a virtual network named VNet1.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains a policy-based virtual network gateway named GW1 and a virtual network named VNet1. You need to ensure that you can configure a point-to-site connection from an on-premises computer to VNet1. Which two actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
options: A. Add a service endpoint to VNet1
C. Create a route-based virtual network gateway
D. Add a connection to GW1
questions.explanation: The correct answers are C (Create a route-based virtual network gateway) and E (Delete GW1). Point-to-site VPN connections require a route-based VPN gateway. Since GW1 is policy-based, it must be deleted first (you cannot have two VPN gateways in the same VNet), and then a new route-based gateway must be created. Option A (service endpoint) is incorrect because service endpoints are for Azure services, not VPN. Option B (reset GW1) is incorrect because resetting does not change the gateway type. Option D (add connection) is incorrect because a policy-based gateway does not support P2S.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have a virtual network named VNet1 as shown in the exhibit. (Click the Exhibit tab.)
questions.questionText: You have a virtual network named VNet1 as shown in the exhibit. (Click the Exhibit tab.) No devices are connected to VNet1. You plan to peer VNet1 to another virtual network named VNet2. VNet2 has an address space of 10.2.0.0/16. You need to create the peering. What should you do first?
options: A. Modify the address space of VNet1.
B. Add a gateway subnet to VNet1.
C. Create a subnet on VNet1 and VNet2.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Modify the address space of VNet1). Virtual network peering requires non-overlapping IP address spaces. The exhibit shows VNet1 has an address space of 10.2.0.0/16, which is the same as VNet2 (10.2.0.0/16). Since they overlap, VNet1's address space must be changed before peering can be established. Option B (gateway subnet) is not required for VNet peering. Option C (create subnets) is not necessary for establishing peering.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have the Azure virtual machines shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have the Azure virtual machines shown in the following table. VNET1 is linked to a private DNS zone named contoso.com that contains the records shown in the following table. You need to ping VM2 from VM1. Which DNS names can you use to ping VM2?
options: B. comp1.contoso.com, comp2.contoso.com, comp3.contoso.com, and comp4.contoso.com
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (comp1.contoso.com, comp2.contoso.com, comp3.contoso.com, and comp4.contoso.com). If all DNS records in the private zone resolve to VM2's IP address, then any of those names can be used to ping VM2. The private DNS zone is linked to the VNet, so all records are resolvable from VM1. Options A, C, and D are too restrictive.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have five Azure virtual machines that run Windows Server 2016. The virtual machines are configured as web servers.
questions.questionText: You have five Azure virtual machines that run Windows Server 2016. The virtual machines are configured as web servers. You have an Azure load balancer named LB1 that provides load balancing services for the virtual machines. You need to ensure that visitors are serviced by the same web server for each request. What should you configure?
options: A. Session persistence to Client IP and protocol
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Session persistence to Client IP and protocol). To ensure visitors are always served by the same web server, you configure session persistence (also known as session affinity or sticky sessions). Setting session persistence to 'Client IP and protocol' ensures that requests from the same client IP and protocol are directed to the same backend VM. Option B (UDP protocol) is incorrect because web traffic uses TCP, not UDP. Option C (persistence to None) is incorrect because None means no session affinity.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that uses the public IP addresses shown in the following table.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that uses the public IP addresses shown in the following table. You need to create a public Azure Standard Load Balancer. Which public IP addresses can you use?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C (IP3 only). A Standard Load Balancer requires Standard SKU public IP addresses. IP3 is the only Standard SKU static IP address. Basic SKU IP addresses cannot be used with Standard Load Balancers. Option A includes Basic SKU IPs. Option B includes only one Basic SKU IP.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription. You are deploying an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster that will contain multiple pods. The pods will use kubernet networking. You need to restrict network traffic between the pods. What should you configure on the AKS cluster?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B (the Calico network policy). When using kubenet networking in AKS, only Calico network policies are supported (not Azure network policies). Azure network policies require Azure CNI networking. Calico provides network policy enforcement for both kubenet and Azure CNI. Option A (Azure network policy) is incorrect because it requires Azure CNI, not kubenet. Option C (pod security policies) is incorrect because these control pod security contexts, not network traffic between pods.

questions.topic: Topic 5 - You have an Azure subscription that contains two virtual machines named VM1 and VM2.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains two virtual machines named VM1 and VM2. You create an Azure load balancer. You plan to create a load balancing rule that will load balance HTTPS traffic between VM1 and VM2. Which two additional load balancer resources should you create before you can create the load balancing rule? Each correct answer presents part of the solution. NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
questions.explanation: The correct answers are A (a frontend IP address) and D (a backend pool).
